name: Mitch's Fish Market & Sushi Bar
tagline: Fresh-off-the-boat seafood right next to the fishing pier — sashimi doesn't get more local than this.
mustOrder: Sashimi platter — whatever's freshest that day, caught that morning
Garlic shrimp — simple and garlicky, local style
Poke bowl — fresh ahi, rice, and furikake
parking: Parking lot at Pier 38. Easy to find, usually has spots.
talkStory: Mitch's is literally next to the Honolulu Fish Auction at Pier 38 — the fish here was swimming yesterday. It's not fancy — plastic chairs and fluorescent lights — but the sashimi is as fresh as it gets without catching it yourself. Great for a no-frills seafood lunch. The fish auction tours happen early morning next door if you're into that. Lunch is the main event; they close in the afternoon.
